channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korea’s parliament passes landmark ban on production and sales of dog meat
item.description: South Korea’s parliament has passed a landmark ban on production and sales of dog meat, as public calls for a prohibition have grown sharply over concerns about animal rights and the country’s international image.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The return of bullfighting to Mexico's capital excites fans and upsets animal rights groups
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In the center of a desolate and cold Plaza Mexico stadium, a young matador raises a red cape and leaps to the right as he gets charged — not by a bull, but by a bull’s head on a cart.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US forces in Middle East attacked more than 8 times before Secretary Lloyd Austin resumed duties in hospital
item.description: American forces in Syria and Iraq were attacked 8 times before Secretary of Defense Lloyd Austin resumed his duties while in the hospital due to an undisclosed ailment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The US and UK say Bangladesh's elections extending Hasina's rule were not credible
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — The United States and the United Kingdom said the elections that extended Bangladesh Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina’s rule were not credible, free and fair.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hong Kongers in Taiwan firmly support the ruling party after watching China erode freedoms at home
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — As Taiwan’s presidential election approaches, many immigrants from Hong Kong, witnesses to the alarming erosion of civil liberties at home, are supporting the ruling Democratic Progressive Party.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG and JOHNSON LAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Migrant caravan regroups in Mexico after government promise of papers falls through
item.description: ARRIAGA, Mexico (AP) — A caravan of about 2,000 migrants on Monday resumed their journey through southern Mexico, after participants were left without the papers the Mexican government appeared to have promised.
item.dc:creator: ÉDGAR H. CLEMENTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangladesh's democracy is strained as Hasina wins a 4th term in a boycotted, low turnout vote
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina has swept to power for a fourth consecutive term in Bangladesh, following an election on Sunday that was boycotted by opposition parties and marked by violent protests and international scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: KRUTIKA PATHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis calls for a universal ban on surrogacy. He says it exploits mother and child
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis called Monday for a universal ban on what he called the “despicable” practice of surrogate motherhood, as he included the “commercialization” of pregnancy in an annual speech listing threats to global peace and human dignity.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 25 killed and 6 injured in collision between minibus and truck in Brazil's northeast
item.description: A horrific accident in Brazil's northeastern region in Bahia state left 25 dead and 6 injured, as a truck collided head on with a minibus transporting tourists back from the beach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Threatened with impeachment, Mayorkas defends record at border
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas touted his record managing the U.S.-Mexico border and blamed Congress for legislative paralysis Monday, days ahead of the first House impeachment hearing against him.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: US secretary of state rallies Mideast leaders to prepare for Gaza's post-war future
item.description: AL ULA, Saudi Arabia (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said Monday that four key Arab nations and Turkey have agreed to begin planning for the reconstruction and governance of Gaza once Israel’s war against Hamas ends.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the major flashpoints in US battle against Iranian proxies
item.description: Since late last year, various Iranian-backed groups have struck U.S. troop positions in Iraq and Syria, tried to bomb merchant vessels in Middle East waterways, and aimed rockets at the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Belarus declines observation invite for parliamentary elections
item.description: Belarus announced on Monday that it will not invite the Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe to monitor its Feb. 25 elections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Andrew Tate wins appeal in Romanian court over seized assets
item.description: Controversial social media influencer Andrew Tate has won an appeal in Romanian court to reverse an earlier decision to seize his assets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 300 arrested in pro-Palestinian protests blocking NYC bridges, Holland Tunnel
item.description: The New York Police Department (NYPD) said Monday it arrested 325 pro-Palestinian protesters blocking three bridges in the New York area, as well as the Holland Tunnel. NYPD Chief of Patrol John Chell said demonstrators began blocking the structures at 9:40 a.m., and that by 11:15 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Nigerian president suspends Poverty Alleviation minister over claims of questionable banking practices
item.description: Nigerian President Bola Tinubu suspended Humanitarian Affairs and Poverty Alleviation Minister Betta Edu on Monday over her alleged use of a private bank account for state transactions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Idris Elba pushes UK for 'absolute ban' on zombie knives, machetes after spike in attacks
item.description: Actor Idris Elba is leading a campaign against knife crime in Britain, saying his country is “at a boiling point.” The “Luther” star launched his Don’t Stop Your Future initiative on Monday with a demonstration outside Parliament in London, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: A notorious Ecuadorian gang leader vanishes from prison and authorities investigate if he escaped
item.description: A convicted leader of one of the most powerful drug gangs in Ecuador has vanished from the prison where he was serving his sentence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ban on swastikas, Nazi salutes goes into effect in Australia
item.description: A ban on the public display of swastika symbols and the Nazi salute in Australia went into effect Monday. The first-of-its-kind law was passed last month as a response to rising antisemitism and hate crimes in the country, especially after the onset of the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, The Hill

item.title: World is more hopeful for 2024: Report
item.description: Public sentiment on AI is divided with 56% expecting positive outcomes such as AI-assisted medical practices while 64% globally anticipate job losses.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine racks up wins against Russia in the Black Sea
item.description: In the Black Sea, Ukraine forced the Russian fleet to retreat from the historic headquarters of Sevastopol in Crimea after hitting ships and key buildings repeatedly with drones and missiles.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: A woman in her 90s is rescued alive 5 days after Japan's deadly earthquake
item.description: WAJIMA, Japan (AP) — A woman in her 90s was pulled alive from a collapsed house in western Japan late Saturday, 124 hours after a major quake slammed the region, killing at least 126 people, toppling buildings and setting off landslides.
item.dc:creator: HIRO KOMAE, AYAKA MCGILL and YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hezbollah, Israel trade heavy cross-border fire as Blinken seeks to prevent regional escalation
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel and Lebanon-based Hezbollah traded fire Saturday in one of the heaviest days of cross-border fighting in recent weeks, a day after the militia's leader urged retaliation for the targeted killing, presumably by Israel, of a top Hamas leader in Lebanon’s capital.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, SAMY MAGDY and NAJIB JOBAIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Real-life 'Ratatouille' restaurant reopens, overlooking Paris 2024 Olympics and reborn Notre Dame
item.description: The Tour d’Argent already boasts a 320,000-bottle wine cellar, a world-famous duck recipe and a storied 441-year history.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israeli defense minister lays out vision for next steps of Gaza war ahead of Blinken visit
item.description: RAFAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel's defense minister on Thursday laid out his vision for the next phase of the war in Gaza, describing how Israeli forces would shift to an apparently scaled-down “ new combat approach” in northern Gaza, while continuing to fight Hamas in the south of the territory “for
item.dc:creator: NAJIB JOBAIN, JULIA FRANKEL and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russia has used North Korean ballistic missiles in Ukraine and is seeking Iranian missiles, US says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. intelligence officials have determined that Russia has acquired ballistic missiles from North Korea and is seeking close-range ballistic missiles from Iran as Moscow struggles to replenish supplies for its war with Ukraine, the White House said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Islamic State group claims responsibility for Iran suicide bombings killing at least 84 people
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The Islamic State group claimed responsibility Thursday for two suicide bombings targeting a commemoration for an Iranian general slain in a 2020 U.S. drone strike, the worst militant attack to strike Iran in decades as the wider Middle East remains on edge.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Airstrike in central Baghdad kills Iran-backed militia leader as regional tensions escalate
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — A U.S. airstrike on the headquarters of an Iran-backed militia in central Baghdad on Thursday killed a high-ranking militia commander, militia officials said.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Top Hamas official Saleh Arouri, who headed West Bank operations, killed in Beirut blast
item.description: Related video: Hostages exchanged between Israel, Hamas BEIRUT (AP) — An explosion in Beirut on Tuesday killed Saleh Arouri, a top official with the Palestinian militant group Hamas and three others, officials with Hamas and the Lebanese group Hezbollah said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
